questions.question: From which play is the quote 'To be, or not to be: that is the question'?
Which Shakespeare play features the characters of Oberon and Titania?
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Out, damned spot! out, I say!"
Which Shakespeare play is set in the city of Verona?
In which play does the character Iago appear?
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Friends, Romans, countrymen, lend me your ears"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Romeo, Romeo! wherefore art thou Romeo?"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "All the world's a stage"
questions.answer: As You Like It, Act 2, Scene 7

questions.question: Play, Act and Scene for the line "Double, double toil and trouble"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Now is the winter of our discontent"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "A horse! a horse! my kingdom for a horse!"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Is this a dagger which I see before me?"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "The quality of mercy is not strained"
questions.answer: The Merchant of Venice, Act 4, Scene 1

questions.question: Play, Act and Scene for the line "If music be the food of love, play on"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "What's in a name? That which we call a rose"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Once more unto the breach, dear friends"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Good night, good night! Parting is such sweet sorrow"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Beware the ides of March"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "The course of true love never did run smooth"
questions.answer: A Midsummer Night's Dream, Act 1, Scene 1

questions.question: Play, Act and Scene for the line "Brevity is the soul of wit"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Cowards die many times before their deaths"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Et tu, Brute?"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "All that glitters is not gold"
questions.answer: The Merchant of Venice, Act 2, Scene 7

questions.question: Play, Act and Scene for the line "The lady doth protest too much, methinks"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "We are such stuff as dreams are made on"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Though this be madness, yet there is method in't"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Cry 'Havoc!' and let slip the dogs of war"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Some are born great, some achieve greatness"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "The fault, dear Brutus, is not in our stars"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "This above all: to thine own self be true"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Something is rotten in the state of Denmark"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "A plague o' both your houses!"
Play, Act and Scene for the line "Lord, what fools these mortals be!"
questions.answer: A Midsummer Night's Dream, Act 3, Scene 2

questions.question: Play, Act and Scene for the line "Get thee to a nunnery"

What is the meaning of the phrase to seek a 'pound of flesh'?
questions.answer: To demand something that is owed, even if it causes harm to the debtor (originating from The Merchant of Venice)
